title: mentha sylvestris var. undulata
intro: Mentha sylvestris var. undulata, also known as wild mint or crinkled mint, is a variety of mint plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It is characterized by its wrinkled leaves and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of menthol and other essential oils. Wild mint can be consumed as a herbal tea, used in topical applications such as creams and ointments, or added to food and drinks to provide health benefits such as digestive relief and relief from headaches, as well as to freshen breath.
